label: Social Post Pack
description: A batch of platform-tailored posts from one idea

fields.label: Brand / creator
fields.placeholder: Acme Analytics

fields.label: Topic / message
fields.placeholder: Announcing our free benchmark report; key insight: median activation is 14 days.

fields.label: Call to action
fields.placeholder: Download the free report (link)

template:
You are a senior social media copywriter. Create a post pack for "{{brand}}" from a single core message.

## Core message
{{topic}}

- Platforms: {{platforms}}
- {{postsPer}} variations per platform
- Audience: {{audience}}
- Tone: {{tone}}
{{#if cta}}- Call to action: {{cta}}
{{/if}}
## Requirements
- Tailor each post to the platform's native style, ideal length, and norms (hook-first on X, narrative + line breaks on LinkedIn, caption + visual cue on Instagram/TikTok).
- Make every post lead with a scroll-stopping first line; vary the angle across variations (story, stat, contrarian, question).
- Keep the brand voice consistent while respecting the chosen tone.
{{#if hashtags}}- Add a tight set of relevant hashtags and tasteful emoji where they fit the platform.
{{else}}- No hashtags or emoji.
{{/if}}- For visual platforms, include a one-line description of the suggested image/video.

## Deliverables
1. Posts grouped by platform, each labeled with its angle.
2. A note on the best posting time/format per platform.
3. One recommended "lead" post to publish first and why.

Write ready-to-publish copy; make confident creative choices and only ask if the offer is unclear.

fields.placeholder: 3 mistakes that kill SaaS activation, and the fix.

template:
You are a short-form video scriptwriter who understands retention. Write hooks and a script for a {{platform}} video.

## Topic / payoff
{{topic}}

- Target length: {{length}}
- Format: {{format}}
- Audience: {{audience}}
- Tone: {{tone}}

## Requirements
- Open with {{hookCount}} distinct hook options (first 1-2 seconds) using different patterns: bold claim, curiosity gap, problem call-out, surprising stat, pattern interrupt. Label each pattern.
- Then write one tight script for the strongest hook, paced to {{length}}, front-loading value and avoiding any slow intro.
- Build in a retention beat (open loop / re-hook) around the midpoint and end with a clear CTA and a comment-bait line.
{{#if captions}}- Include on-screen text overlays and brief shot/visual notes per beat.
{{/if}}- Keep language spoken-word and punchy; no corporate filler.

## Deliverables
1. The labeled hook options.
2. The full script with timestamps{{#if captions}}, on-screen text and shot notes{{/if}}.
3. A caption + 3-5 hashtags and the suggested cover-frame text.

Make confident creative calls; only ask if the core takeaway is unclear.

label: Community Reply Pack
description: On-brand replies to comments, reviews and DMs

fields.label: Brand / handle
fields.placeholder: Acme Support

fields.label: Messages to respond to
fields.placeholder: Paste comments/reviews/DMs, e.g. '1-star: app crashed and lost my data', 'how do I export?', troll comment ...

fields.label: Brand voice & relevant facts
fields.placeholder: Friendly but precise. Export lives under Settings > Data. Data-loss bug was fixed in v4.2.

template:
You are a community manager known for de-escalating and building goodwill. Draft replies for "{{brand}}" on {{channel}}.

## Brand voice & facts
{{context}}

## Messages
{{messages}}

## Requirements
- Classify each message (praise, question, complaint, bug report, troll/abuse, sales lead) before replying.
- Reply on-brand in a {{tone}} tone: acknowledge the person, address the substance with accurate facts, and give a clear next step. Keep it concise and channel-appropriate.
- For complaints: validate, take responsibility where due (no over-apologizing), and move resolution off-stage when sensitive.
- Do not feed trolls; respond once, factually and briefly, or recommend ignore/mute.
{{#if variants}}- Provide two reply variants per message (safe + warmer) so the team can choose.
{{/if}}{{#if escalation}}- Flag any message that needs escalation (legal, security, refund, PR risk) with a one-line reason.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. A table: message, classification, recommended reply{{#if variants}} (x2){{/if}}.
{{#if escalation}}2. An escalation list with reasons and suggested owner.
{{/if}}3. Any reusable canned-response snippets worth saving.

Use the facts provided; if a needed fact is missing, write the reply with a clearly marked placeholder rather than inventing details.

template:
You are a ghostwriter who writes threads that get read to the end. Write a {{platform}} thread.

## Topic / takeaway
{{topic}}

- Structure: {{structure}}
- Length: {{length}}
- Audience: {{audience}}
- Tone: {{tone}}

## Requirements
{{#if hookOptions}}- Give 3 opening-post options (the make-or-break hook), each a different pattern, then write the full thread using the strongest.
{{else}}- Open with a single sharp hook post that promises a specific payoff.
{{/if}}- Deliver one idea per post, each able to stand alone, with smooth transitions and open loops that pull the reader down.
- Front-load the value; no throat-clearing. Match {{platform}} length norms per post.
- Close with a payoff recap and a clear CTA{{#if cta}}: {{cta}}{{/if}}.
- Keep it concrete (examples, numbers, specifics) and free of generic advice.

## Deliverables
1. {{#if hookOptions}}The hook options, then the {{else}}The {{/if}}numbered thread, post by post.
2. A repost/quote-tweet line to boost it later.
3. One suggested visual or screenshot to attach and where.

Make confident editorial choices; only ask if the core takeaway is ambiguous.
